content_markdown: What is the definition of the unified atomic mass unit?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the question.

options.label: A
options.text: The mass of one atom of hydrogen.

options.label: B
options.text: $\frac{1}{12}$ of the mass of an atom of carbon-12.

options.label: C
options.text: The mass of one atom of carbon-12.

options.label: D
options.text: $\frac{1}{16}$ of the mass of an atom of oxygen-16.

vibe_explanation: The unified atomic mass unit ($u$) is defined by international agreement as exactly one-twelfth of the mass of a neutral atom of carbon-12 in its ground state. This provides a standard scale for comparing the relative masses of different isotopes.
